pr.title: Preprocess lcf and start purging lcf pointers
pr.author: RevoSucks

summary: Made the linker command file preprocessable and began replacing hard-coded LCF section addresses with derived segment symbols/macros. The Makefile now generates build/ldscript.lcf via cpp, uses that generated script for linking, adds a default/all target, enables gcc-style MWCC diagnostics, runs elf2dol more verbosely, and simplifies clean to remove the build directory. The linker script now defines BEGIN_SEG/END_SEG macros, chains most section starts from prior segment ends, adds temporary hacks for sdata/sdata2 positioning, and computes _SDA_BASE_/_SDA2_BASE_ from linker-derived positions instead of fixed literal addresses. A matching-support symbol _SDA_BASE_START was added in asm/sdata2.s, and newline-only cleanup occurred in include/global.h and asm/bss.s.
what_changed: Changed LDSCRIPT from the source ldscript.lcf to $(BUILD_DIR)/ldscript.lcf so linking consumes a generated/preprocessed linker script.
Added CPP := cpp -P and a Makefile rule that preprocesses ldscript.lcf into the build directory with dependency output via -MMD -MP -MT and -MF.
Added default: all and all: $(DOL) targets, making the DOL build the explicit default path.
Added -msgstyle gcc to CFLAGS, likely to produce GCC-style diagnostics from MWCC.
Changed elf2dol invocation from $(ELF2DOL) $< $@ to $(ELF2DOL) -v -v $< $@ for more verbose conversion output.
Changed clean from deleting individual outputs to recursively removing build, then cleaning tools.
Reworked ldscript.lcf to use preprocessor macros BEGIN_SEG(name, addr) and END_SEG(name) for defining _<segment>SegmentStart and _<segment>SegmentEnd symbols around sections.

Replaced many fixed BIND addresses in ldscript.lcf with chained segment positioning, e.g. text begins at _extabindex_SegmentEnd, ctors at _textSegmentEnd, dtors at _ctorsSegmentEnd, rodata at _dtorsSegmentEnd, data at _rodataSegmentEnd, and bss at _dataSegmentEnd.
Kept the init section special-cased with ADDR_START 0x80003100 because both the start symbol and BIND address need that value.

Moved extab_ and extabindex_ earlier in the script and generated their positions from preceding segment ends, while preserving TODO comments that their section names should become extab and extabindex.
Added explicit comments documenting temporary hacks for sdata and sdata2 placement because sbss is not recognized by elf2dol and alignment was not working as desired.
Changed _SDA_BASE_ and _SDA2_BASE_ from fixed literals to expressions based on _sdata2SegmentStartHack and _sdata2SegmentEnd.
Added global label _SDA_BASE_START at lbl_804DB6A0 in asm/sdata2.s.
Removed or fixed missing trailing-newline issues in asm/bss.s and include/global.h.
smart_moves: Preprocessing ldscript.lcf lets the project introduce macros and symbolic layout formulas without manually duplicating section-boundary boilerplate.
Generating the linker script under $(BUILD_DIR) keeps the source LCF as the canonical template while making derived output disposable via clean.
Using BEGIN_SEG/END_SEG creates consistent start/end symbols for sections, which reduces reliance on brittle hard-coded addresses and supports future linker-script maintenance.
Chaining section placement from prior segment ends is a concrete step toward purging raw LCF pointers while preserving deterministic section order.

The PR documents known ugly hacks directly in ldscript.lcf, especially around sbss, sdata, sdata2, elf2dol, and alignment, which helps future contributors avoid mistaking these formulas for final clean solutions.
Adding _SDA_BASE_START as an assembly-global label at the existing lbl_804DB6A0 location provides a named anchor that can be reused by linker expressions or future cleanup.
decomp_lessons: For linker-script cleanup, prefer deriving segment addresses from previous segment end symbols where possible instead of pinning every section to a magic address.
When an LCF needs macros, preprocess it in the build directory rather than requiring the linker to understand preprocessor syntax directly.
If matching requires temporary layout hacks, name them conspicuously, keep them near the affected sections, and explain the tool limitation or unresolved alignment issue.
Small-data base symbols are especially sensitive: changing _SDA_BASE_ and _SDA2_BASE_ should be tied to concrete section anchors and verified by the final DOL SHA1.
Verbose conversion/linking tools can be useful during layout refactors because failures may come from DOL conversion, section binding, or linker script preprocessing rather than C/ASM compilation.
naming_conventions: Segment-boundary linker symbols use the pattern _<name>SegmentStart and _<name>SegmentEnd, such as _initSegmentStart, _textSegmentEnd, _dataSegmentEnd, and _sdata2SegmentEnd.
The LCF macro names are uppercase with underscores: BEGIN_SEG, END_SEG, and ADDR_START.
Temporary linker-script workaround symbols are explicitly marked with Hack suffixes, e.g. _sdataSegmentStartHack and _sdata2SegmentStartHack.
The small-data anchor added in assembly uses the conventional global name _SDA_BASE_START and is colocated with existing lbl_804DB6A0.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Added _SDA_BASE_START as an alternate global label at the existing sdata2 location lbl_804DB6A0, allowing symbolic references without moving bytes.

Computed _SDA_BASE_ as _sdata2SegmentStartHack + 0x3CC0 and _SDA2_BASE_ as _sdata2SegmentEnd + 0xDE0, reducing direct dependence on absolute addresses while maintaining expected small-data base values.
Used linker-script section chaining to preserve layout while making address relationships explicit.
Retained a manual ADDR_START 0x80003100 for init because the diff notes both bind and start need to use it.
Preserved matching verification through the existing SHA1 check after elf2dol.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that remove _sdataSegmentStartHack or _sdata2SegmentStartHack from ldscript.lcf.
Find later PRs that rename extab_ and extabindex_ to extab and extabindex as noted by TODO comments.
Search for subsequent changes to _SDA_BASE_, _SDA2_BASE_, and _SDA_BASE_START to see how small-data base handling stabilized.
Search for build-system changes around preprocessing ldscript.lcf and dependency includes for generated .d files.
